name: Notice of Appearance of Counsel
summary: Drafts a formal Notice of Appearance of Counsel to notify the court and parties of an attorney's representation in litigation. Extracts case details, ensures compliance with federal, state, or local rules including caption formatting and service requirements. Use when an attorney enters representation, substitutes counsel, or joins existing counsel in criminal defense or other litigation matters.
content:
# Notice of Appearance of Counsel - Professional Workflow

You are tasked with drafting a Notice of Appearance of Counsel, a formal litigation document that officially notifies the court and all parties that an attorney is entering representation for a specific party in pending litigation. This document establishes the attorney-client relationship in the court's record, triggers procedural obligations for service of future filings, and must comply with all applicable rules of civil procedure, local court rules, and jurisdictional requirements.

## Initial Information Gathering and Document Review

Begin by searching through any uploaded case documents to identify critical information needed for the notice. Extract the complete case caption including the full court name with district or division, all party names exactly as they appear in the complaint or answer, the assigned case or docket number, and any judge assignment. Locate the existing counsel of record to ensure proper service and identify whether this appearance represents initial representation, substitution of counsel, or additional counsel joining existing representation. Review any prior appearances or representation orders to understand the procedural posture and ensure compliance with rules governing attorney changes.

Verify the specific jurisdictional requirements by identifying whether this is a federal court matter governed by the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, a state court proceeding subject to state-specific rules, or a specialized tribunal with unique requirements. Each jurisdiction maintains distinct formatting conventions, filing procedures, and substantive requirements that must be precisely followed. If the jurisdiction is unclear from the documents, determine the appropriate court based on the case type and parties involved.

## Court Caption and Formatting Compliance

Construct the court caption with meticulous attention to the jurisdiction's formatting requirements. The caption must reflect the exact styling conventions used by the specific court, including proper abbreviations for court divisions, correct placement of case numbers and judge assignments, and adherence to margin, spacing, and font specifications. Federal courts typically require the district and division in the court name, the case number in a specific format with year and sequence, and may require judge initials or full name depending on local rules. State courts vary significantly, with some requiring department numbers, case type designations, or category codes within the caption itself.

Research the local rules through the court's official website or electronic filing system to confirm caption requirements, as improper formatting can result in rejection of the filing or administrative delays. Pay particular attention to how the jurisdiction handles multiple parties, consolidated cases, or related proceedings, as these situations may require modified caption formats or cross-referencing to other case numbers.

## Notice Statement and Legal Foundation

Draft the substantive notice statement using clear, formal language that unambiguously establishes the attorney's entry of appearance. The statement should reference the specific rule of civil procedure authorizing the appearance, such as Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 11 or the corresponding state rule, and should follow established legal conventions for appearance notices. The typical formulation reads: "PLEASE TAKE NOTICE that [Attorney Full Name], of [Law Firm Complete Name], hereby enters an appearance as counsel of record for [Party Name], [Plaintiff/Defendant/Respondent/Petitioner], in the above-captioned matter, pursuant to [applicable rule citation]."

When the appearance involves substitution or replacement of prior counsel, incorporate appropriate language addressing the withdrawal or substitution process. Verify whether the jurisdiction requires a separate motion for substitution of counsel, consent from the client and withdrawing counsel, or court approval before the substitution becomes effective. Some courts permit substitution by notice alone, while others mandate a formal motion with supporting documentation. If prior counsel has not yet withdrawn, coordinate the timing of filings to ensure continuous representation and avoid gaps that could prejudice the client's interests.

## Comprehensive Attorney and Firm Information

Provide complete contact information formatted according to court requirements and professional standards. Include the attorney's full legal name exactly as registered with the state bar, the complete law firm name without abbreviations unless commonly recognized, the firm's physical street address rather than post office boxes unless specifically permitted by local rule, primary telephone number with area code, fax number if the court requires or if commonly used for service, and email address designated for electronic service under the jurisdiction's e-filing rules.

Verify and include the attorney's bar admission number for the specific jurisdiction where the case is pending. For federal court appearances, confirm whether a separate federal bar admission number is required or whether state bar membership suffices. If appearing pro hac vice, clearly designate this status and reference the order granting admission for this specific case. Some jurisdictions require designation of lead counsel versus local counsel, particularly when out-of-state attorneys appear with local co-counsel to satisfy jurisdictional requirements.

Confirm the email address provided is actively monitored and authorized for electronic service under the court's electronic filing rules. Many jurisdictions now require electronic service as the primary method, and the email address listed in the notice becomes the official service address for all future filings. Include the attorney's electronic filing system registration number if required by local rules, and specify the preferred method of service when the court permits options between electronic, mail, or personal delivery.

## Party Representation Specification

Identify with precision the party or parties for whom the attorney is appearing, using exact names and designations as they appear in the case caption and pleadings. Specify the capacity in which each party appears, whether as an individual, corporate entity, trustee, executor, guardian, or other representative capacity. For business entities, verify the correct legal name through corporate records or secretary of state databases to ensure accuracy, as misnaming a corporate party can create jurisdictional issues or service problems.

When representing multiple parties, list each separately and clarify whether the representation is joint or individual, particularly when potential conflicts of interest might arise. For government entities, confirm the proper designation and determine whether special rules apply to service, appearance requirements, or sovereign immunity considerations. If the party is appearing through a representative capacity, ensure that appropriate documentation of authority exists in the case file, such as letters testamentary for executors or corporate resolutions for officers acting on behalf of entities.

## Authentication and Signature Requirements

Construct the signature block in compliance with the court's authentication requirements, which vary significantly between paper and electronic filing systems. For traditional paper filings, provide space for the attorney's original signature in blue or black ink, the date of signing, and the attorney's printed name and bar number beneath the signature line. For electronic filings, follow the jurisdiction's specific requirements for electronic signatures, which may include typing the attorney's name between forward slashes (/s/ Attorney Name/), using a digital signature certificate, uploading a scanned signature page, or employing the electronic filing system's built-in signature function.

Include a statement confirming the attorney's admission to practice in the jurisdiction or pro hac vice status if applicable. Some courts require this attestation within the signature block itself, while others expect it in the body of the notice. Verify whether the court requires the signature to appear on the same page as the notice text or permits continuation to a separate signature page, as some jurisdictions have specific rules about signature page formatting to prevent fraudulent additions to signed documents.

## Certificate of Service and Compliance

Conclude with a properly formatted certificate of service that certifies delivery of the notice to all parties or their counsel of record. The certificate must specify the exact date of service, the method of service employed (electronic mail, U.S. Mail, personal delivery, or other court-authorized method), and the complete names and addresses of all persons or entities served. For electronic service, include email addresses and confirm that service complies with the jurisdiction's electronic filing and service rules, including any requirements for confirmation of receipt or service during specific hours.

The certificate should employ formal language such as: "I hereby certify that on [specific date], a true and correct copy of the foregoing Notice of Appearance of Counsel was served upon all counsel of record and pro se parties via [specific method of service] at the addresses listed below." Follow this with a complete service list showing each recipient's name, firm or designation, complete address, and email address if service was electronic. Some jurisdictions require the certificate to be signed separately from the main document or to include additional attestations about the completeness and accuracy of the service list.

## Document Assembly, Quality Control, and Filing

Assemble the complete document ensuring all required sections are present, properly ordered, and formatted consistently with local rules regarding page numbering, margins (typically one inch on all sides), font size (usually 12-point in Times New Roman or similar), and line spacing (double-spaced text or as specified by local rule). Include any required footer or header information such as case number, attorney identification, or page numbers in the format prescribed by the court.

Conduct a thorough review for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with all applicable rules before filing. Verify that party names are spelled identically to the caption, that the case number is correct, that all contact information is current and accurate, and that the certificate of service includes all necessary recipients. Confirm the appropriate filing method, whether through the court's electronic filing system, by paper filing with the clerk's office, or both if required by local rule or the court's transition to electronic filing.

Determine any filing fees associated with the notice of appearance and ensure payment is submitted through the appropriate method. While many jurisdictions do not charge fees for notices of appearance, some courts assess filing fees for any document that initiates attorney involvement. Retain a conformed copy showing the court's filing stamp with date and time for the case file, and immediately calendar any deadlines triggered by the appearance, including responsive pleading deadlines if this is an initial appearance for a defendant, discovery deadlines, and any pending motion or hearing dates that now require the appearing attorney's attention.

Search the uploaded documents for any standing orders, case management orders, or scheduling orders that impose obligations on counsel of record, as the entry of appearance typically subjects the attorney to all existing case deadlines and requirements. Notify the client of the successful filing and confirm that all future court communications will be directed to the appearing attorney's contact information as listed in the notice.
